verses.latin_vulgate: Et addidit furor Domini irasci contra Israël, commovitque David in eis dicentem : Vade, numera Israël et Judam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙סֶף֙ אַף־יְהֹוָ֔ה לַחֲר֖וֹת בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַיָּ֨סֶת אֶת־דָּוִ֤ד בָּהֶם֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר לֵ֛ךְ מְנֵ֥ה אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל וְאֶת־יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the anger of the Lord was again kindled against Israel, and stirred up David among them, saying: Go, number Israel and Juda.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herran viha taas julmistui Israelia vastaan ja hän kehoitti Davidin heidän keskellensä, sanoen: mene ja lue Israel ja Juuda.
verses.king_james: And again the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and he moved David against them to say, Go, number Israel and Judah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque rex ad Joab principem exercitus sui : Perambula omnes tribus Israël a Dan usque Bersabee, et numerate populum, ut sciam numerum ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ אֶל־יוֹאָ֣ב ׀ שַׂר־הַחַ֣יִל אֲשֶׁר־אִתּ֗וֹ שֽׁוּט־נָ֞א בְּכׇל־שִׁבְטֵ֤י יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ מִדָּן֙ וְעַד־בְּאֵ֣ר שֶׁ֔בַע וּפִקְד֖וּ אֶת־הָעָ֑ם וְיָ֣דַעְתִּ֔י אֵ֖ת מִסְפַּ֥ר הָעָֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And the king said to Joab the general of his army: Go through all the tribes of Israel from Dan to Bersabee, and number ye the people that I may know the number of them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas sanoi Joabille sodanpäämiehellensä: mene nyt kaikkein Israelin sukukuntain ympäri, ruveten Danista BerSebaan asti, ja lue kansa, että minä tietäisin kansan paljouden.
verses.king_james: For the king said to Joab the captain of the host, which was with him, Go now through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan even to Beer–sheba, and number ye the people, that I may know the number of the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Joab regi : Adaugeat Dominus Deus tuus ad populum tuum, quantus nunc est, iterumque centuplicet in conspectu domini mei regis : sed quid sibi dominus meus rex vult in re hujuscemodi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יוֹאָ֜ב אֶל־הַמֶּ֗לֶךְ וְיוֹסֵ֣ף יְהֹוָה֩ אֱלֹהֶ֨יךָ אֶל־הָעָ֜ם כָּהֵ֤ם ׀ וְכָהֵם֙ מֵאָ֣ה פְעָמִ֔ים וְעֵינֵ֥י אֲדֹנִֽי־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ רֹא֑וֹת וַאדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ לָ֥מָּה חָפֵ֖ץ בַּדָּבָ֥ר הַזֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Joab said to the king: The Lord thy God increase thy people, and make them as many more as they are now, and again multiply them a hundredfold in the sight of my lord the king: but what meaneth my lord the king by this kind of thing?
verses.finnish_biblia: Joab sanoi kuninkaalle: Herra sinun Jumalas enetköön siihen kansaan sata kertaa sen verran kuin siinä nyt on, että herrani kuninkaan silmät sen näkisivät; mutta mitä iloa on herrallani kuninkaalla siitä asiasta?
verses.king_james: And Joab said unto the king, Now the Lord thy God add unto the people, how many soever they be, an hundredfold, and that the eyes of my lord the king may see it: but why doth my lord the king delight in this thing?

verses.latin_vulgate: Obtinuit autem sermo regis verba Joab et principum exercitus : egressusque est Joab et princeps militum a facie regis, ut numerarent populum Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֶּחֱזַ֤ק דְּבַר־הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ אֶל־יוֹאָ֔ב וְעַ֖ל שָׂרֵ֣י הֶחָ֑יִל וַיֵּצֵ֨א יוֹאָ֜ב וְשָׂרֵ֤י הַחַ֙יִל֙ לִפְנֵ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ לִפְקֹ֥ד אֶת־הָעָ֖ם אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the king's words prevailed over the words of Joab, and of the captains of the army: and Joab, and the captains of the soldiers went out from the presence of the king, to number the people of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuninkaan sana vahvistui Joabia ja sodanpäämiehiä vastaan. Niin Joab ja sotajoukon päämiehet menivät kuninkaan tyköä lukemaan Israelin kansaa.
verses.king_james: Notwithstanding the king’s word prevailed against Joab, and against the captains of the host. And Joab and the captains of the host went out from the presence of the king, to number the people of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque pertransissent Jordanem, venerunt in Aroër ad dexteram urbis, quæ est in valle Gad :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעַבְר֖וּ אֶת־הַיַּרְדֵּ֑ן וַיַּחֲנ֣וּ בַעֲרוֹעֵ֗ר יְמִ֥ין הָעִ֛יר אֲשֶׁ֛ר בְּתוֹךְ־הַנַּ֥חַל הַגָּ֖ד וְאֶל־יַעְזֵֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when they had passed the Jordan, they came to Aroer to the right side of the city, which is in the vale of Gad.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he menivät Jordanin ylitse ja sioittivat itsensä Aroeriin, oikialle puolelle sitä paikkaa, joka on keskellä Gadin ojaa, Jaeserin tykönä;
verses.king_james: And they passed over Jordan, and pitched in Aroer, on the right side of the city that lieth in the midst of the river of Gad, and toward Jazer:

verses.latin_vulgate: et per Jazer transierunt in Galaad, et in terram inferiorem Hodsi, et venerunt in Dan silvestria. Circumeuntesque juxta Sidonem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֙אוּ֙ הַגִּלְעָ֔דָה וְאֶל־אֶ֥רֶץ תַּחְתִּ֖ים חׇדְשִׁ֑י וַיָּבֹ֙אוּ֙ דָּ֣נָה יַּ֔עַן וְסָבִ֖יב אֶל־צִידֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And by Jazer they passed into Galaad, and to the lower land of Hodsi, and they came into the woodlands of Dan. And going about by Sidon,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tulivat Gileadiin ja alhaiseen maahan Hadsiin, sitte tulivat he DanJaniin ja Zidonia ympärinsä;
verses.king_james: Then they came to Gilead, and to the land of Tahtim–hodshi; and they came to Dan–jaan, and about to Zidon,

verses.latin_vulgate: transierunt prope mœnia Tyri, et omnem terram Hevæi et Chananæi, veneruntque ad meridiem Juda in Bersabee :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֙אוּ֙ מִבְצַר־צֹ֔ר וְכׇל־עָרֵ֥י הַחִוִּ֖י וְהַֽכְּנַעֲנִ֑י וַיֵּ֥צְא֛וּ אֶל־נֶ֥גֶב יְהוּדָ֖ה בְּאֵ֥ר שָֽׁבַע׃
verses.douay_rheims: They passed near the walls of Tyre, and all the land of the Hevite, and the Chanaanite, and they came to the south of Juda into Bersabee:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tulivat vahvaan kaupunkiin Tyroon ja kaikkiin Heviläisten ja Kanaanealaisten kaupunkeihin. Sitte he läksivät sieltä etelän puolelle Juudaa BerSebaan asti;
verses.king_james: And came to the strong hold of Tyre, and to all the cities of the Hivites, and of the Canaanites: and they went out to the south of Judah, even to Beer–sheba.

verses.latin_vulgate: et lustrata universa terra, affuerunt post novem menses et viginti dies in Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּשֻׁ֖טוּ בְּכׇל־הָאָ֑רֶץ וַיָּבֹ֜אוּ מִקְצֵ֨ה תִשְׁעָ֧ה חֳדָשִׁ֛ים וְעֶשְׂרִ֥ים י֖וֹם יְרוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And having gone through the whole land, after nine months and twenty days, they came to Jerusalem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kävivät kaiken maan ympäri, ja tulivat yhdeksän kuukauden ja kahdenkymmenen päivän perästä Jerusalemiin jällensä.
verses.king_james: So when they had gone through all the land, they came to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dedit ergo Joab numerum descriptionis populi regi, et inventa sunt de Israël octingenta millia virorum fortium qui educerent gladium, et de Juda quingenta millia pugnatorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֵּ֥ן יוֹאָ֛ב אֶת־מִסְפַּ֥ר מִפְקַד־הָעָ֖ם אֶל־הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וַתְּהִ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֡ל שְֽׁמֹנֶה֩ מֵא֨וֹת אֶ֤לֶף אִֽישׁ־חַ֙יִל֙ שֹׁ֣לֵֽף חֶ֔רֶב וְאִ֣ישׁ יְהוּדָ֔ה חֲמֵשׁ־מֵא֥וֹת אֶ֖לֶף אִֽישׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Joab gave up the sum of the number of the people to the king, and there were found of Israel eight hundred thousand valiant men that drew the sword: and of Juda five hundred thousand fighting men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Joab antoi kuninkaalle kansan luvun, ja Israelissa oli kahdeksansataa tuhatta vahvaa miekan ulosvetävää sotamiestä; mutta Juudassa oli viisisataa tuhatta miestä.
verses.king_james: And Joab gave up the sum of the number of the people unto the king: and there were in Israel eight hundred thousand valiant men that drew the sword; and the men of Judah were five hundred thousand men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Percussit autem cor David eum, postquam numeratus est populus : et dixit David ad Dominum : Peccavi valde in hoc facto : sed precor, Domine, ut transferas iniquitatem servi tui, quia stulte egi nimis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤ךְ לֵב־דָּוִד֙ אֹת֔וֹ אַחֲרֵי־כֵ֖ן סָפַ֣ר אֶת־הָעָ֑ם {פ}וַיֹּ֨אמֶר דָּוִ֜ד אֶל־יְהֹוָ֗ה חָטָ֤אתִֽי מְאֹד֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשִׂ֔יתִי וְעַתָּ֣ה יְהֹוָ֗ה הַֽעֲבֶר־נָא֙ אֶת־עֲוֺ֣ן עַבְדְּךָ֔ כִּ֥י נִסְכַּ֖לְתִּי מְאֹֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: But David's heart struck him, after the people were numbered: and David said to the Lord: I have sinned very much in what I have done: but I pray thee, O Lord, to take away the iniquity of thy servant, because I have done exceeding foolishly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Davidin sydän tykytti sitte kuin kansa luettu oli, ja David sanoi Herralle: minä olen sangen suuresti syntiä tehnyt, että minä sen tein. Herra, käännä nyt palvelias pahateko pois; sillä minä olen tehnyt sangen tyhmästi.
verses.king_james: And David’s heart smote him after that he had numbered the people. And David said unto the Lord, I have sinned greatly in that I have done: and now, I beseech thee, O Lord, take away the iniquity of thy servant; for I have done very foolishly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surrexit itaque David mane, et sermo Domini factus est ad Gad prophetam et videntem David, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֥קׇם דָּוִ֖ד בַּבֹּ֑קֶר {פ}וּדְבַר־יְהֹוָ֗ה הָיָה֙ אֶל־גָּ֣ד הַנָּבִ֔יא חֹזֵ֥ה דָוִ֖ד לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David arose in the morning, and the word of the Lord came to Gad the prophet and the seer of David, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin David huomeneltain nousi, tuli Herran sana prophetan Gadin Davidin näkiän tykö, sanoen:
verses.king_james: For when David was up in the morning, the word of the Lord came unto the prophet Gad, David’s seer, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Vade, et loquere ad David : Hæc dicit Dominus : Trium tibi datur optio : elige unum quod volueris ex his, ut faciam tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָל֞וֹךְ וְדִבַּרְתָּ֣ אֶל־דָּוִ֗ד כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה שָׁלֹ֕שׁ אָנֹכִ֖י נוֹטֵ֣ל עָלֶ֑יךָ בְּחַר־לְךָ֥ אַחַת־מֵהֶ֖ם וְאֶעֱשֶׂה־לָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Go, and say to David: Thus saith the Lord: I give thee thy choice of three things, choose one of them which thou wilt, that I may do it to thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mene ja puhu Davidille: näin sanoo Herra: kolme minä panen sinun etees; valitse itselles niistä yksi, jonka minä sinulle teen.
verses.king_james: Go and say unto David, Thus saith the Lord, I offer thee three things; choose thee one of them, that I may do it unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque venisset Gad ad David, nuntiavit ei, dicens : Aut septem annis veniet tibi fames in terra tua : aut tribus mensibus fugies adversarios tuos, et ille te persequentur : aut certe tribus diebus erit pestilentia in terra tua. Nunc ergo delibera, et vide quem respondeam ei qui me misit sermonem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹא־גָ֥ד אֶל־דָּוִ֖ד וַיַּגֶּד־ל֑וֹ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ל֡וֹ הֲתָב֣וֹא לְךָ֣ שֶׁבַע־שָׁנִ֣ים ׀ רָעָ֣ב ׀ בְּאַרְצֶ֡ךָ אִם־שְׁלֹשָׁ֣ה חֳ֠דָשִׁ֠ים נֻסְךָ֨ לִפְנֵי־צָרֶ֜יךָ וְה֣וּא רֹדְפֶ֗ךָ וְאִם־הֱ֠י֠וֹת שְׁלֹ֨שֶׁת יָמִ֥ים דֶּ֙בֶר֙ בְּאַרְצֶ֔ךָ עַתָּה֙ דַּ֣ע וּרְאֵ֔ה מָה־אָשִׁ֥יב שֹׁלְחִ֖י דָּבָֽר׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And when Gad was come to David, he told him, saying: Either seven years of famine shall come to thee in thy land: or thou shalt flee three months before thy adversaries, and they shall pursue thee: or for three days there shall be a pestilence in thy land. Now therefore deliberate, and see what answer I shall return to him that sent me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Gad tuli Davidin tykö, ilmoitti ja sanoi hänelle: tahdotkos että nälkä tulee seitsemäksi ajastajaksi maakunnalles, taikka ettäs kolme kuukautta pakenet vihamiestes edellä ja he ajavat sinua takaa, eli että rutto on kolme päivää sinun maallas? Niin ota nyt vaari, ja katso, mitä minä hänelle vastaan, joka minun lähetti.
verses.king_james: So Gad came to David, and told him, and said unto him, Shall seven years of famine come unto thee in thy land? or wilt thou flee three months before thine enemies, while they pursue thee? or that there be three days’ pestilence in thy land? now advise, and see what answer I shall return to him that sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem David ad Gad : Coarctor nimis : sed melius est ut incidam in manus Domini (multæ enim misericordiæ ejus sunt) quam in manus hominum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֧אמֶר דָּוִ֛ד אֶל־גָּ֖ד צַר־לִ֣י מְאֹ֑ד נִפְּלָה־נָּ֤א בְיַד־יְהֹוָה֙ כִּֽי־רַבִּ֣ים רַחֲמָ֔ו וּבְיַד־אָדָ֖ם אַל־אֶפֹּֽלָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to Gad: I am in a great strait: but it is better that I should fall into the hands of the Lord (for his mercies are many) than into the hands of men.
verses.finnish_biblia: David sanoi Gadille: minulla on sangen suuri ahdistus; mutta salli meidän langeta Herran käsiin, sillä Herran armo on suuri. En minä tahdo langeta ihmisten käsiin.
verses.king_james: And David said unto Gad, I am in a great strait: let us fall now into the hand of the Lord; for his mercies are great: and let me not fall into the hand of man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Immisitque Dominus pestilentiam in Israël, de mane usque ad tempus constitutum, et mortui sunt ex populo a Dan usque ad Bersabee septuaginta millia virorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֵּ֨ן יְהֹוָ֥ה דֶּ֙בֶר֙ בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל מֵהַבֹּ֖קֶר וְעַד־עֵ֣ת מוֹעֵ֑ד וַיָּ֣מׇת מִן־הָעָ֗ם מִדָּן֙ וְעַד־בְּאֵ֣ר שֶׁ֔בַע שִׁבְעִ֥ים אֶ֖לֶף אִֽישׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord sent a pestilence upon Israel, from the morning unto the time appointed, and there died of the people from Dan to Bersabee seventy thousand men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra antoi ruttotaudin tulla Israeliin, ruveten aamulla niin määrättyyn aikaan asti; ja kansaa kuoli Danista niin BerSebaan asti, seitsemänkymmentä tuhatta miestä.
verses.king_james: So the Lord sent a pestilence upon Israel from the morning even to the time appointed: and there died of the people from Dan even to Beer–sheba seventy thousand men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque extendisset manum suam angelus Domini super Jerusalem ut disperderet eam, misertus est Dominus super afflictione, et ait angelo percutienti populum : Sufficit : nunc contine manum tuam. Erat autem angelus Domini juxta aream Areuna Jebusæi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַח֩ יָד֨וֹ הַמַּלְאָ֥ךְ ׀ יְֽרוּשָׁלַ֘͏ִם֮ לְשַׁחֲתָהּ֒ וַיִּנָּ֤חֶם יְהֹוָה֙ אֶל־הָ֣רָעָ֔ה וַ֠יֹּ֠אמֶר לַמַּלְאָ֞ךְ הַמַּשְׁחִ֤ית בָּעָם֙ רַ֔ב עַתָּ֖ה הֶ֣רֶף יָדֶ֑ךָ וּמַלְאַ֤ךְ יְהֹוָה֙ הָיָ֔ה עִם־גֹּ֖רֶן (האורנה) [הָֽאֲרַ֥וְנָה] הַיְבֻסִֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And when the angel of the Lord had stretched out his hand over Jerusalem to destroy it, the Lord had pity on the affliction, and said to the angel that slew the people: It is enough: now hold thy hand. And the angel of the Lord was by the thrashingfloor of Areuna the Jebusite.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin enkeli ojensi kätensä Jerusalemin ylitse, kadottaaksensa häntä, niin Herra katui sitä pahaa ja sanoi enkelille, joka kansaa hävitti: kyllä jo on; pidä nyt kätes alallansa. Ja Herran enkeli oli Araunan Jebusilaisen riihen tykönä.
verses.king_james: And when the angel stretched out his hand upon Jerusalem to destroy it, the Lord repented him of the evil, and said to the angel that destroyed the people, It is enough: stay now thine hand. And the angel of the Lord was by the threshingplace of Araunah the Jebusite.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque David ad Dominum cum vidisset angelum cædentem populum : Ego sum qui peccavi, ego inique egi : isti qui oves sunt, quid fecerunt ? vertatur, obsecro, manus tua contra me, et contra domum patris mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֩אמֶר֩ דָּוִ֨ד אֶל־יְהֹוָ֜ה בִּרְאֹת֣וֹ ׀ אֶֽת־הַמַּלְאָ֣ךְ ׀ הַמַּכֶּ֣ה בָעָ֗ם וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ הִנֵּ֨ה אָנֹכִ֤י חָטָ֙אתִי֙ וְאָנֹכִ֣י הֶעֱוֵ֔יתִי וְאֵ֥לֶּה הַצֹּ֖אן מֶ֣ה עָשׂ֑וּ תְּהִ֨י נָ֥א יָדְךָ֛ בִּ֖י וּבְבֵ֥ית אָבִֽי׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to the Lord, when he saw the angel striking the people: It is I; I am he that have sinned, I have done wickedly: these that are the sheep, what have they done? let thy hand, I beseech thee, be turned against me, and against my father's house.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin David näki enkelin, joka kansaa löi, sanoi hän Herralle: katso, minä olen syntiä tehnyt ja minä olen sen pahan tehnyt: mitä nämät lampaat ovat tehneet? Anna siis kätes olla minua vastaan ja minun isäni huonetta vastaan.
verses.king_james: And David spake unto the Lord when he saw the angel that smote the people, and said, Lo, I have sinned, and I have done wickedly: but these sheep, what have they done? let thine hand, I pray thee, be against me, and against my father’s house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit autem Gad ad David in die illa, et dixit ei : Ascende, et constitue altare Domino in area Areuna Jebusæi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹא־גָ֥ד אֶל־דָּוִ֖ד בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֑וּא וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ל֗וֹ עֲלֵה֙ הָקֵ֤ם לַֽיהֹוָה֙ מִזְבֵּ֔חַ בְּגֹ֖רֶן (ארניה) [אֲרַ֥וְנָה] הַיְבֻסִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Gad came to David that day, and said: Go up, and build an altar to the Lord in the thrashingfloor of Areuna the Jebusite.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sinä päivänä tuli Gad Davidin tykö ja sanoi hänelle: mene ja rakenna Herralle alttari Jebusilaisen Araunan riiheen.
verses.king_james: And Gad came that day to David, and said unto him, Go up, rear an altar unto the Lord in the threshingfloor of Araunah the Jebusite.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ascendit David juxta sermonem Gad, quem præceperat ei Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤עַל דָּוִד֙ כִּדְבַר־גָּ֔ד כַּאֲשֶׁ֖ר צִוָּ֥ה יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David went up according to the word of Gad which the Lord had commanded him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David meni kohta niinkuin Gad sanoi ja Herra oli käskenyt.
verses.king_james: And David, according to the saying of Gad, went up as the Lord commanded.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conspiciensque Areuna, animadvertit regem et servos ejus transire ad se :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּשְׁקֵ֣ף אֲרַ֗וְנָה וַיַּ֤רְא אֶת־הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ וְאֶת־עֲבָדָ֔יו עֹבְרִ֖ים עָלָ֑יו וַיֵּצֵ֣א אֲרַ֔וְנָה וַיִּשְׁתַּ֧חוּ לַמֶּ֛לֶךְ אַפָּ֖יו אָֽרְצָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Areuna looked, and saw the king and his servants coming towards him:
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuin Arauna käänsi itsensä, näki hän kuninkaan palvelioinensa tulevan tykönsä; niin Arauna läksi ulos ja lankesi kasvoillensa maahan kuninkaan eteen,
verses.king_james: And Araunah looked, and saw the king and his servants coming on toward him: and Araunah went out, and bowed himself before the king on his face upon the ground.

verses.latin_vulgate: et egressus adoravit regem prono vultu in terram, et ait : Quid causæ est ut veniat dominus meus rex ad servum suum ? Cui David ait : Ut emam a te aream, et ædificem altare Domino, et cesset interfectio quæ grassatur in populo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֲרַ֔וְנָה מַדּ֛וּעַ בָּ֥א אֲדֹנִֽי־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ אֶל־עַבְדּ֑וֹ וַיֹּ֨אמֶר דָּוִ֜ד לִקְנ֧וֹת מֵעִמְּךָ֣ אֶת־הַגֹּ֗רֶן לִבְנ֤וֹת מִזְבֵּ֙חַ֙ לַֽיהֹוָ֔ה וְתֵעָצַ֥ר הַמַּגֵּפָ֖ה מֵעַ֥ל הָעָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And going out he worshipped the king, bowing with his face to the earth, and said: Wherefore is my lord the king come to his servant? And David said to him: To buy the thrashingfloor of thee, and build an altar to the Lord, that the plague, which rageth among the people, may cease.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanoi: miksi herrani kuningas tulee palveliansa tykö? David sanoi: riihtä ostamaan sinulta, rakentaakseni Herralle alttaria, että vitsaus lakkais kansasta.
verses.king_james: And Araunah said, Wherefore is my lord the king come to his servant? And David said, To buy the threshingfloor of thee, to build an altar unto the Lord, that the plague may be stayed from the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Areuna ad David : Accipiat, et offerat dominus meus rex sicut placet ei : habes boves in holocaustum, et plaustrum, et juga boum in usum lignorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֲרַ֙וְנָה֙ אֶל־דָּוִ֔ד יִקַּ֥ח וְיַ֛עַל אֲדֹנִ֥י הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ הַטּ֣וֹב בְּעֵינָ֑ו רְאֵה֙ הַבָּקָ֣ר לָעֹלָ֔ה וְהַמֹּרִגִּ֛ים וּכְלֵ֥י הַבָּקָ֖ר לָעֵצִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Areuna said to David: Let my lord the king take, and offer, as it seemeth good to him: thou hast here oxen for a holocaust, and the wain, and the yokes of the oxen for wood.
verses.finnish_biblia: Arauna sanoi Davidille: herrani kuningas ottakaan ja uhratkaan niinkuin hänelle kelpaa. Katso, tässä on härkä polttouhriksi, reet ja kaikki härkäin kalut haloiksi.
verses.king_james: And Araunah said unto David, Let my lord the king take and offer up what seemeth good unto him: behold, here be oxen for burnt sacrifice, and threshing instruments and other instruments of the oxen for wood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia dedit Areuna rex regi : dixitque Areuna ad regem : Dominus Deus tuus suscipiat votum tuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַכֹּ֗ל נָתַ֛ן אֲרַ֥וְנָה הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ לַמֶּ֑לֶךְ {ס} וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֲרַ֙וְנָה֙ אֶל־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ יְהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהֶ֖יךָ יִרְצֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: All these things Areuna as a king gave to the king: and Areuna said to the king: The Lord thy God receive thy vow.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nämät kaikki, kuningas, antaa Arauna kuninkaalle. Ja Arauna sanoi kuninkaalle: Herra sinun Jumalas leppyköön sinulle.
verses.king_james: All these things did Araunah, as a king, give unto the king. And Araunah said unto the king, The Lord thy God accept thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui respondens rex, ait : Nequaquam ut vis, sed emam pretio a te, et non offeram Domino Deo meo holocausta gratuita. Emit ergo David aream, et boves, argenti siclis quinquaginta :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ אֶל־אֲרַ֗וְנָה לֹ֚א כִּֽי־קָנ֨וֹ אֶקְנֶ֤ה מֵאֽוֹתְךָ֙ בִּמְחִ֔יר וְלֹ֧א אַעֲלֶ֛ה לַיהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהַ֖י עֹל֣וֹת חִנָּ֑ם וַיִּ֨קֶן דָּוִ֤ד אֶת־הַגֹּ֙רֶן֙ וְאֶת־הַבָּקָ֔ר בְּכֶ֖סֶף שְׁקָלִ֥ים חֲמִשִּֽׁים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king answered him, and said: Nay, but I will buy it of thee, at a price, and I will not offer to the Lord my God holocausts free cost. So David bought the floor, and the oxen, for fifty sicles of silver:
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuningas sanoi Araunalle: ei suinkaan, vaan minä ostan sen täydellisesti sinulta hinnalla; sillä en minä tee Herralle polttouhria siitä, joka minulle lahjaksi on annettu. Niin osti David riihen ja härjän viidelläkymmenellä hopiasiklillä,
verses.king_james: And the king said unto Araunah, Nay; but I will surely buy it of thee at a price: neither will I offer burnt offerings unto the Lord my God of that which doth cost me nothing. So David bought the threshingfloor and the oxen for fifty shekels of silver.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ædificavit ibi David altare Domino, et obtulit holocausta et pacifica : et propitiatus est Dominus terræ, et cohibita est plaga ab Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֩בֶן֩ שָׁ֨ם דָּוִ֤ד מִזְבֵּ֙חַ֙ לַיהֹוָ֔ה וַיַּ֥עַל עֹל֖וֹת וּשְׁלָמִ֑ים וַיֵּעָתֵ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ לָאָ֔רֶץ וַתֵּעָצַ֥ר הַמַּגֵּפָ֖ה מֵעַ֥ל יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David built there an altar to the Lord, and offered holocausts and peace offerings: and the Lord became merciful to the land, and the plague was stayed from Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja rakensi Herralle siinä alttarin ja uhrasi polttouhreja ja kiitosuhreja; ja Herra leppyi maakunnalle, ja vitsaus lakkasi Israelissa.
verses.king_james: And David built there an altar unto the Lord, and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings. So the Lord was intreated for the land, and the plague was stayed from Israel.
